content.question: Would structural conversion of 6/8-year tracks to a uniform 6+6 system, or reallocation of resources to 4-year gimnáziums, materially reduce socioeconomic segregation in Hungarian secondary education, or would free primary-school choice sustain segregation through other channels?
content.reduction_path: Model counterfactual school composition under track elimination using linked primary-school choice data; compare segregation indices under current, restructured, and fully inclusive scenarios; document complementary segregation mechanisms (residential sorting, charter/private schools, feeder-school networks)
